summary: Additive pipeline abstraction over existing AnalyserNode wiring. No architectural concerns — the plan extends two existing modules with a thin, zero-allocation wrapper and exposes it through the established AudioPlayer interface. All changes are backward-compatible and already implemented.

new_abstractions.name: AnalyserPipeline
new_abstractions.justification: Encapsulates pre-allocated buffers and a poll() method to eliminate per-frame Uint8Array allocations in the render loop. This is the correct layer for this concern — it sits between the raw AnalyserNode and the render loop consumer.

modified_interfaces.name: AudioPlayer
modified_interfaces.risk: Low. Additive method on an existing interface. No existing callers break.

consistency_with_existing_architecture.module_boundaries: Respected. Pipeline lives in the audio layer where analysis already exists. No cross-layer leakage.
consistency_with_existing_architecture.privacy_model: No storage APIs used. No new data exposed. Verified by dedicated tests.
consistency_with_existing_architecture.performance_model: Aligned with CLAUDE.md guidance: pre-allocated buffers avoid per-frame object churn.
consistency_with_existing_architecture.audio_graph_design: Preserves existing source → AnalyserNode → GainNode → destination chain. Pipeline reads from AnalyserNode without modifying the graph.

acceptance_criteria_coverage.Audio source is connected to an Analyser: Covered by existing createAnalyser() + connectSource wiring, verified in tests.
acceptance_criteria_coverage.Frequency data is readable per frame: Covered by pipeline.poll() filling pre-allocated frequency buffer.
acceptance_criteria_coverage.Time-domain data is readable per frame: Covered by pipeline.poll() filling pre-allocated timeDomain buffer.
acceptance_criteria_coverage.Mute state does not disable analysis: Covered by GainNode being downstream of AnalyserNode; verified by muted-state tests in both test files.

risks.description: Mock fidelity: getByteFrequencyData/getByteTimeDomainData mocks fill arrays in place, matching real Web Audio API behavior. If mocks diverge, tests could pass while real behavior differs.
risks.mitigation: Mock implementations correctly use arr.fill() pattern matching the real API's in-place buffer write semantics.
fftSize is set once in createAnalyser() and never mutated. Web Audio API guarantees frequencyBinCount = fftSize / 2.

notes: Implementation and tests are already complete in the working tree. The plan is fully realized — steps 1-4 are done. Only step 5 (verify: run tests and build) remains to confirm everything passes.
